Teoria Typescript

//declarando variaveis const username: string = "Anne"; let age: number = 26; var passwordIsCorrect: boolean = false; //declarando arr const arr: Array = [1, 2, 3]; const array: number[] = [1, 2, 3]; //declarando obj const person: { name: string; age: number } = { name: "Wesliane", age: 26, }; //any - var pode assumir qqr valor: string, number, boolean let aux: any; aux = "aux"; aux = 0; aux = true; //parametros tipados function add(n1: number, n2: number): number { return n1 + n2; } //void: function que nao retorna nada // parametros opcinais: declarados usando ? function sayHello(name?: string): void { console.log("Hello,", name || "World"); } //tipando methods usamos arrow finction(adaptada) type person = { name: string; age: number; sayHello: (name?: string) => void; }; //tipando callbacks function method(condition: boolean, callback: () => void): void { if (condition) { callback(); } }
